sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. שָׂרַךְ שָׂרַג to intertwine, to plait, whence שׂרֵק, שׂרֵקָה, שְׂרוּקִּים, shoots, tendrils, pr.n. מַשְׂרֵקָה.

(2) i.q. Syr. ܣܪܰܩ, סְרַק to comb, to clean out flax by combing, Isa 19:9. This signification may be so connected with the former, that it may pr. be to disentangle any thing tangled or entwined; comp. as to the power of some verbs, Gramm. § 51, 2.

Derivatives, שׂרֵק, שְׂרוּקִּים and pr.n. מַשְׂרֵקָה.

II. שָׂרֹק to be reddish, tawny, whence-
